purpose: Defines the `!retry [dashboardId] {task_id}` command for re-dispatching a single failed task. Validates task exists, has a progress file (must be failed for proceed; in_progress/completed require confirmation), all upstream deps are completed (warns if a dep failed), saves the previous failure summary, deletes the old progress file, reads plan.json, ANALYZES THE FAILURE ROOT CAUSE (reads previous logs, identifies cause: missing file/type mismatch/wrong pattern/env issue/ambiguity, reads relevant files), then dispatches a new agent with PREVIOUS FAILURE/ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS/REMEDIATION GUIDANCE/PREVIOUS WORKER LOGS sections.
gotchas: Pending tasks (no progress file) cannot be retried — direct user to !dispatch instead
Failed dependencies are warned about, not auto-retried — the user must !retry the dep first to unblock
in_progress and completed status retries require user confirmation before proceeding
The master MUST analyze the failure root cause BEFORE re-dispatching — do not blindly retry; read previous logs, identify cause, read relevant files
For complex failures requiring a diagnostic-first approach, dispatch a repair worker via failed_task.md protocol instead of !retry
REMEDIATION GUIDANCE in the retry prompt should include SPECIFIC fixes (correct import paths, function signatures) — vague guidance defeats the purpose
patterns: 13-step procedure: parse → read init → read progress → validate (existence+status+deps) → save previous summary → delete old progress → read plan → analyze root cause → resolve project_root → dispatch with retry sections → log → display confirmation
Retry prompt sections: PREVIOUS FAILURE → ROOT CAUSE ANALYSIS → REMEDIATION GUIDANCE → PREVIOUS WORKER LOGS
Validation order: existence first, then status, then dependencies
conventions: Numbered steps with embedded code blocks for the retry prompt sections and bash commands
Trailing footnote redirecting complex failures to failed_task.md / repair worker protocol
